[These two verses were evidently added after Bhattotpala had
written his Vivfti on this work. In the last verse to get at the intend-
ed meaning we have to take the negative particle't' with both
the preceding and following words.]

Chapter XXIX—Prognostics from Flowers and Creepers
Regarding plants and prognostics see the 'Reader's Digest',
May 1972, pp. 70-72, "Do Plants Have Feelings" by James Lincoln
Collier, who refers to the experiments conducted by Backster and
other scientists wuh the polygraph. These experiments show that
even modern science has come to believe that plants have feelings,
can read man's thoughts and forecast coming events.
By observing the abundant growth of flowers and fruits of
tfees, the availability of all things in large quantities at cheap rates
should be ascertained; so is the excellent growth of crops.
